title: Design authorized security validation layer
body:
Design the layer that performs safe, scoped, authorized checks against target systems.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define AssessmentTarget schema
- [ ] Define AssessmentScope schema
- [ ] Define ValidationJob schema
- [ ] Define ValidationCheck schema
- [ ] Define ScanResult schema
- [ ] Define safety policy integration
- [ ] Define evidence linkage
- [ ] Define how validation results become Findings

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Architecture documented in docs/authorized-security-validation.md
- [ ] Validation workflow documented
- [ ] No-exploit default policy is included
- [ ] Validation output feeds SOC, GRC, remediation, and knowledge workflows

title: Implement assessment scope and authorization model
body:
Ensure validation jobs only run against explicitly authorized targets.

Tasks:
- [ ] Add AssessmentTarget schema
- [ ] Add AssessmentScope schema
- [ ] Add allowed targets field
- [ ] Add allowed ports and protocols
- [ ] Add validity window
- [ ] Add safety level
- [ ] Add rate limit field
- [ ] Add authorization record

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Validation jobs cannot run without an active scope
- [ ] Targets outside scope are blocked
- [ ] Expired scopes are rejected
- [ ] Authorization decisions are auditable

title: Implement scanner orchestrator skeleton
body:
Create a service that schedules and tracks validation checks.

Tasks:
- [ ] Create ValidationJob object
- [ ] Create ValidationCheck queue model
- [ ] Integrate with Flow runtime
- [ ] Integrate with ToolCall model
- [ ] Add status tracking
- [ ] Add error handling
- [ ] Add audit logging

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A validation job can be created from a scope
- [ ] Checks can be queued and status-tracked
- [ ] ToolCall and Evidence linkage is supported

title: Implement safe service discovery checks
body:
Perform read-only service discovery on authorized targets.

Tasks:
- [ ] Add host reachability check
- [ ] Add allowed port discovery
- [ ] Add service fingerprinting placeholder
- [ ] Add banner capture placeholder
- [ ] Store raw output as evidence
- [ ] Enforce rate limiting
- [ ] Block targets outside scope

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Only authorized targets are checked
- [ ] Discovery is read-only
- [ ] Results are stored as evidence
- [ ] Findings can be generated from discovery results

title: Implement web security baseline checks
body:
Check common web security posture without exploitation.

Tasks:
- [ ] Check security headers
- [ ] Check TLS configuration
- [ ] Check HTTP methods
- [ ] Check redirect behavior
- [ ] Check CORS configuration
- [ ] Detect directory listing signal
- [ ] Store evidence for each check

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Web baseline checks are non-destructive
- [ ] Missing or risky configurations generate findings
- [ ] Evidence links are created for every finding

title: Integrate validation findings with external intelligence
body:
Enrich discovered services and versions with NVD, CISA KEV, EPSS, GHSA, OSV, and vendor advisory data.

Tasks:
- [ ] Map service/version to CVE candidates
- [ ] Enrich CVE candidates with CISA KEV status
- [ ] Enrich CVE candidates with EPSS score
- [ ] Link enrichment to evidence
- [ ] Feed enrichment into risk scoring
- [ ] Preserve source references

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Validation findings can use external intelligence context
- [ ] Risk scoring can consume enrichment
- [ ] Missing or low-confidence enrichment does not create false certainty

title: Convert validation results into normalized findings
body:
Convert scanner and validation results into normalized Finding objects.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define ValidationFinding mapping
- [ ] Add finding severity
- [ ] Add finding confidence
- [ ] Add evidence linkage
- [ ] Add asset linkage
- [ ] Add control mapping candidate
- [ ] Add remediation candidate

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Validation output becomes normalized Finding objects
- [ ] Findings link to target, asset, evidence, and validation job
- [ ] Findings can be consumed by SOC, GRC, and remediation workflows

title: Define and enforce no-exploit default policy
body:
Document and enforce that validation is non-destructive and no-exploit by default.

Tasks:
- [ ] Document no exploit execution by default
- [ ] Document no brute force
- [ ] Document no credential attacks
- [ ] Document no payload upload
- [ ] Document no lateral movement
- [ ] Document no destructive checks
- [ ] Define future exception model
- [ ] Add blocked action audit behavior

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Policy documented in docs/validation-safety-policy.md
- [ ] Unsafe actions are blocked by default
- [ ] Future active validation requires explicit policy and approval
